Whenever we return home peacefully, we should acknowledge God’s providence in preserving our going out and coming in. If we have been kept through dangers greater than usual, we are especially obligated to be thankful. Distinctive blessings lay strong obligations upon us. The prophet told Jehoshaphat that he had acted very badly by joining Ahab. He received the rebuke well. See how the rebuke affected him; he carefully searched his own kingdom. By what the prophet said, Jehoshaphat realized that his previous attempts at reformation were pleasing to God; therefore, he did what was left undone. It is good when praise encourages us in our duty. There are different gifts and abilities, but they all come from the same Spirit and are for the public benefit; and as each person has received a gift, let him use it in service. Blessed be God for government officials and ministers, scholars and politicians, men of learning, and men of business. Observe the instructions the king gave: They must do everything in the fear of the Lord, with a sincere and honest heart. And they must constantly strive to prevent sin, as an offense to God, and something that would bring wrath on the people.
